items.summary: The Iowa Developmental Disabilities Council has announced Gov. Kim Reynolds' appointment of five new members - one from the Quad Cities - who bring diverse expe…

items.headline: Riverside Park, Muscatine, will close for late-July events
items.summary: Most of Riverside Park will be closed to vehicular traffic during the final week of July as several major summer events take place along the riverfront in Musca…

items.headline: Iowa State Fair announces over 40 new drinks for 2026
items.summary: Fairgoers can vote for their favorite drinks on the Official Iowa State Fair App between Aug. 13 and Aug. 19.

items.headline: Iowa nonprofit adapts to rising costs to keep seniors fed
items.summary: Amid rising costs for gas and food, WesleyLife is adapting operations to maintain sercices for 14,000 Iowa seniors.

items.headline: RAGBRAI leadership gives city of Perry approval for museum
items.summary: As riders passed through Tuesday, the Perry Iowa Historical Society sold raffle tickets to raise money for the new facility.

items.headline: Jessica VanDyke of Visit Quad Cities earns Forty Under 40 Award

items.headline: Illinois' Blue Envelope Program aims to help drivers with autism during traffic stops
items.summary: Starting Jan. 1, 2027, drivers can get a free blue envelope at their DMV to hold their vehicle documents. Similar programs have been established across the U.S.

items.headline: DMV services returning to Aledo, Illinois Secretary of State confirms
items.summary: Since the previous location's closure last year, Aledo residents have had to travel to DMV facilities in Silvis, Monmouth and Galesburg for in-person services.

items.headline: Semi truck driver airlifted to hospital after crash that landed in Linn County creek
items.summary: According to an Iowa State Patrol crash report, the semi truck suffered a tire failure while traveling north on Interstate 380.

items.headline: Bettendorf expanding park cameras after vandalism causes thousands of dollars in damage
items.summary: Kiwanis Park currently has three security cameras, and city officials are now planning to install additional cameras at other parks.

items.headline: Illinois Tollway seeks public input on $26.5 billion plan
